parameter.name: createdBy_application_displayName
parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date. For example, if a user changes their display name the API might show the new value in a future response, but the items associated with the user don't show up as changed when using delta.

parameter.name: createdBy_application_id
parameter.description: Unique identifier for the identity or actor. For example, in the access reviews decisions API, this property might record the id of the principal, that is, the group, user, or application that's subject to review.

parameter.name: parentReference_driveId
parameter.description: Unique identifier of the drive instance that contains the driveItem. Only returned if the item is located in a drive. Read-only.

parameter.name: parentReference_driveType
parameter.description: Identifies the type of drive. Only returned if the item is located in a drive. See drive resource for values.

parameter.name: parentReference_id
parameter.description: Unique identifier of the driveItem in the drive or a listItem in a list. Read-only.

parameter.name: parentReference_name
parameter.description: The name of the item being referenced. Read-only.

parameter.name: parentReference_path
parameter.description: Percent-encoded path that can be used to navigate to the item. Read-only.

parameter.name: parentReference_shareId
parameter.description: A unique identifier for a shared resource that can be accessed via the Shares API.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_listId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's list in SharePoint.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_listItemId
parameter.description: An integer identifier for the item within the containing list.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_listItemUniqueId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item within OneDrive for Business or a SharePoint site.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_siteId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's site collection (SPSite).

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_siteUrl
parameter.description: The SharePoint URL for the site that contains the item.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_tenantId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the tenancy.

parameter.name: parentReference_sharepointIds_webId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's site (SPWeb).

parameter.name: parentReference_siteId
parameter.description: For OneDrive for Business and SharePoint, this property represents the ID of the site that contains the parent document library of the driveItem resource or the parent list of the listItem resource. The value is the same as the id property of that site resource. It is an opaque string that consists of three identifiers of the site. For OneDrive, this property is not populated.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_externalReplyMessage
parameter.description: The automatic reply to send to the specified external audience, if Status is AlwaysEnabled or Scheduled.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_internalReplyMessage
parameter.description: The automatic reply to send to the audience internal to the signed-in user's organization, if Status is AlwaysEnabled or Scheduled.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledEndDateTime_dateTime
parameter.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation ({date}T{time}; for example, 2017-08-29T04:00:00.0000000).

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledEndDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_dateTime
parameter.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation ({date}T{time}; for example, 2017-08-29T04:00:00.0000000).

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_dateFormat
parameter.description: The date format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_language_displayName
parameter.description: A name representing the user's locale in natural language, for example, 'English (United States)'.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_language_locale
parameter.description: A locale representation for the user, which includes the user's preferred language and country/region. For example, 'en-us'. The language component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 639-1, and the country component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_timeFormat
parameter.description: The time format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_timeZone
parameter.description: The default time zone for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_daysOfWeek
parameter.description: The days of the week on which the user works.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_endTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user stops working.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_startTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user starts working.

parameter.name: createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_timeZone_name
parameter.description: The name of a time zone. It can be a standard time zone name such as 'Hawaii-Aleutian Standard Time', or 'Customized Time Zone' for a custom time zone.

parameter.name: createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: createdByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date. parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_driveId
parameter.description: Unique identifier of the drive instance that contains the driveItem. Only returned if the item is located in a drive. Read-only.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_driveType
parameter.description: Identifies the type of drive. Only returned if the item is located in a drive. See drive resource for values.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_id
parameter.description: Unique identifier of the driveItem in the drive or a listItem in a list. Read-only.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_name
parameter.description: The name of the item being referenced. Read-only.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_path
parameter.description: Percent-encoded path that can be used to navigate to the item. Read-only.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_shareId
parameter.description: A unique identifier for a shared resource that can be accessed via the Shares API.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_listId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's list in SharePoint.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_listItemId
parameter.description: An integer identifier for the item within the containing list.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_listItemUniqueId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item within OneDrive for Business or a SharePoint site.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_siteId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's site collection (SPSite).

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_siteUrl
parameter.description: The SharePoint URL for the site that contains the item.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_tenantId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the tenancy.

parameter.name: list_parentReference_sharepointIds_webId
parameter.description: The unique identifier (guid) for the item's site (SPWeb).

parameter.name: list_parentReference_siteId
parameter.description: For OneDrive for Business and SharePoint, this property represents the ID of the site that contains the parent document library of the driveItem resource or the parent list of the listItem resource. The value is the same as the id property of that site resource. It is an opaque string that consists of three identifiers of the site. For OneDrive, this property is not populated.

parameter.description: The automatic reply to send to the specified external audience, if Status is AlwaysEnabled or Scheduled.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_internalReplyMessage
parameter.description: The automatic reply to send to the audience internal to the signed-in user's organization, if Status is AlwaysEnabled or Scheduled.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledEndDateTime_dateTime
parameter.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation ({date}T{time}; for example, 2017-08-29T04:00:00.0000000).

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledEndDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_dateTime
parameter.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation ({date}T{time}; for example, 2017-08-29T04:00:00.0000000).

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_dateFormat
parameter.description: The date format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_language_displayName
parameter.description: A name representing the user's locale in natural language, for example, 'English (United States)'.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_language_locale
parameter.description: A locale representation for the user, which includes the user's preferred language and country/region. For example, 'en-us'. The language component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 639-1, and the country component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_timeFormat
parameter.description: The time format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_timeZone
parameter.description: The default time zone for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_daysOfWeek
parameter.description: The days of the week on which the user works.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_endTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user stops working.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_startTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user starts working.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_timeZone_name
parameter.description: The name of a time zone. It can be a standard time zone name such as 'Hawaii-Aleutian Standard Time', or 'Customized Time Zone' for a custom time zone.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_presence_statusMessage_publishedDateTime
parameter.description: Time in which the status message was published.Read-only.publishedDateTime isn't available when you request the presence of another user.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_settings_shiftPreferences_createdBy_application_displayName
parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date. For example, if a user changes their display name the API might show the new value in a future response, but the items associated with the user don't show up as changed when using delta.

parameter.name: list_createdByUser_settings_shiftPreferences_createdBy_application_id
parameter.description: Unique identifier for the identity or actor. For example, in the access reviews decisions API, this property might record the id of the principal, that is, the group, user, or application that's subject to review.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: list_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

The value is the same as the id property of that site resource. It is an opaque string that consists of three identifiers of the site. For OneDrive, this property is not populated.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date. For example, if a user changes their display name the API might show the new value in a future response, but the items associated with the user don't show up as changed when using delta.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_dateFormat
parameter.description: The date format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_language_displayName
parameter.description: A name representing the user's locale in natural language, for example, 'English (United States)'.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_language_locale
parameter.description: A locale representation for the user, which includes the user's preferred language and country/region. For example, 'en-us'. The language component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 639-1, and the country component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_timeFormat
parameter.description: The time format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_timeZone
parameter.description: The default time zone for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_daysOfWeek
parameter.description: The days of the week on which the user works.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_endTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user stops working.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_startTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user starts working.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_timeZone_name
parameter.description: The name of a time zone. It can be a standard time zone name such as 'Hawaii-Aleutian Standard Time', or 'Customized Time Zone' for a custom time zone.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.name: root_audio_album
parameter.description: The title of the album for this audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_albumArtist
parameter.description: The artist named on the album for the audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_artist
parameter.description: The performing artist for the audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_bitrate
parameter.description: Bitrate expressed in kbps.

parameter.name: root_audio_composers
parameter.description: The name of the composer of the audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_copyright
parameter.description: Copyright information for the audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_disc
parameter.description: The number of the disc this audio file came from.

parameter.name: root_audio_discCount
parameter.description: The total number of discs in this album.

parameter.name: root_audio_duration
parameter.description: Duration of the audio file, expressed in milliseconds

parameter.name: root_audio_genre
parameter.description: The genre of this audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_hasDrm
parameter.description: Indicates if the file is protected with digital rights management.

parameter.name: root_audio_isVariableBitrate
parameter.description: Indicates if the file is encoded with a variable bitrate.

parameter.name: root_audio_title
parameter.description: The title of the audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_track
parameter.description: The number of the track on the original disc for this audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_trackCount
parameter.description: The total number of tracks on the original disc for this audio file.

parameter.name: root_audio_year
parameter.description: The year the audio file was recorded.

parameter.name: root_bundle_album_coverImageItemId
parameter.description: Unique identifier of the driveItem that is the cover of the album.

parameter.name: root_bundle_childCount
parameter.description: Number of children contained immediately within this container.

parameter.name: root_deleted_state
parameter.description: Represents the state of the deleted item.

parameter.name: root_file_hashes_crc32Hash
parameter.description: The CRC32 value of the file (if available). Read-only.

parameter.name: root_file_hashes_quickXorHash
parameter.description: A proprietary hash of the file that can be used to determine if the contents of the file change (if available). Read-only.

parameter.name: root_file_hashes_sha1Hash
parameter.description: SHA1 hash for the contents of the file (if available). Read-only.

parameter.name: root_file_hashes_sha256Hash
parameter.description: This property isn't supported. Don't use.

parameter.name: root_file_mimeType
parameter.description: The MIME type for the file. This is determined by logic on the server and might not be the value provided when the file was uploaded. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_fileSystemInfo_createdDateTime
parameter.description: The UTC date and time the file was created on a client.

parameter.name: root_fileSystemInfo_lastAccessedDateTime
parameter.description: The UTC date and time the file was last accessed. Available for the recent file list only.

parameter.name: root_fileSystemInfo_lastModifiedDateTime
parameter.description: The UTC date and time the file was last modified on a client.

parameter.name: root_folder_childCount
parameter.description: Number of children contained immediately within this container.

parameter.name: root_folder_view_sortBy
parameter.description: The method by which the folder should be sorted.

parameter.name: root_folder_view_sortOrder
parameter.description: If true, indicates that items should be sorted in descending order. Otherwise, items should be sorted ascending.

parameter.name: root_folder_view_viewType
parameter.description: The type of view that should be used to represent the folder.

parameter.name: root_image_height
parameter.description: Optional. Height of the image, in pixels. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_image_width
parameter.description: Optional. Width of the image, in pixels. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_location_altitude
parameter.description: Optional. The altitude (height), in feet, above sea level for the item. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_location_latitude
parameter.description: Optional. The latitude, in decimal, for the item. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_location_longitude
parameter.description: Optional. The longitude, in decimal, for the item. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_malware_description
parameter.description: Contains the virus details for the malware facet.

parameter.name: root_package_type
parameter.description: A string indicating the type of package. While oneNote is the only currently defined value, you should expect other package types to be returned and handle them accordingly.

parameter.name: root_pendingOperations_pendingContentUpdate_queuedDateTime
parameter.description: Date and time the pending binary operation was queued in UTC time. Read-only.

parameter.name: root_remoteItem_parentReference_siteId
parameter.description: For OneDrive for Business and SharePoint, this property represents the ID of the site that contains the parent document library of the driveItem resource or the parent list of the listItem resource. parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_listItem_createdByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date. parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_automaticRepliesSetting_scheduledStartDateTime_timeZone
parameter.description: Represents a time zone, for example, 'Pacific Standard Time'. See below for more possible values.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_dateFormat
parameter.description: The date format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_language_displayName
parameter.description: A name representing the user's locale in natural language, for example, 'English (United States)'.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_language_locale
parameter.description: A locale representation for the user, which includes the user's preferred language and country/region. For example, 'en-us'. The language component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 639-1, and the country component follows 2-letter codes as defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_timeFormat
parameter.description: The time format for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_timeZone
parameter.description: The default time zone for the user's mailbox.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_daysOfWeek
parameter.description: The days of the week on which the user works.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_endTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user stops working.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_startTime
parameter.description: The time of the day that the user starts working.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_mailboxSettings_workingHours_timeZone_name
parameter.description: The name of a time zone. It can be a standard time zone name such as 'Hawaii-Aleutian Standard Time', or 'Customized Time Zone' for a custom time zone.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignIn
parameter.description: true if the user must change their password on the next sign-in; otherwise false.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_forceChangePasswordNextSignInWithMfa
parameter.description: If true, at next sign-in, the user must perform a multifactor authentication (MFA) before being forced to change their password. The behavior is identical to forceChangePasswordNextSignIn except that the user is required to first perform a multifactor authentication before password change. After a password change, this property will be automatically reset to false. If not set, default is false.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_passwordProfile_password
parameter.description: The password for the user. This property is required when a user is created. It can be updated, but the user will be required to change the password on the next sign-in. The password must satisfy minimum requirements as specified by the user's passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted (either successfully or unsuccessfully) to sign in to the directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains non-interactive sign-ins going back to May 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastNonInteractiveSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user. This property records the last time a user attempted an interactive sign-in to the directory—whether the attempt was successful or not. Note: Since unsuccessful attempts are also logged, this value might not accurately reflect actual system usage. For tracking actual account access, please use the lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime property. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSignInRequestId
parameter.description: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInDateTime
parameter.description: The date and time of the user's most recent successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in. Use this property if you need to determine when the account was truly accessed. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Microsoft Entra ID maintains interactive sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property, see Manage inactive user accounts in Microsoft Entra ID.

parameter.name: root_listItem_lastModifiedByUser_signInActivity_lastSuccessfulSignInRequestId
parameter.description: The request ID of the last successful sign-in.

parameter.description: The display name of the identity.For drive items, the display name might not always be available or up to date.
